title: Box Breathing
description: A short breathing pattern to settle your mind and body.
steps: Sit comfortably and relax your shoulders.
Inhale gently through your nose for 4 counts.
Hold your breath softly for 4 counts.
Exhale slowly for 4 counts, then pause for 4 counts before repeating.

title: Body Scan
description: A guided check-in from head to toe.
steps: Close your eyes or soften your gaze.
Notice your forehead, jaw, and shoulders and release any tension.
Move your attention slowly through your chest, stomach, hips, and legs.
Finish by taking one slow breath and noticing how your body feels now.

title: Five Senses Grounding
description: A grounding exercise to reconnect with the present moment.
steps: Name 5 things you can see around you.
Name 4 things you can physically feel.
Name 3 things you can hear right now.
Name 2 things you can smell and 1 thing you can taste.

title: Gratitude Pause
description: A moment to gently shift attention toward what is good.
steps: Take a slow breath in and out.
Think of one person, place, or memory you appreciate.
Notice what that gratitude feels like in your body.
Say quietly to yourself: Thank you for this moment.

title: Loving Kindness
steps: Place a hand on your heart or rest your hands softly in your lap.
Repeat silently: May I be safe. May I be calm. May I be well.
Bring another person to mind and repeat the same wishes for them.
End by sending those wishes outward to everyone around you.

title: Mindful Walking
description: A light movement practice with full attention on each step.
steps: Stand tall and notice the feeling of your feet on the ground.
Begin walking slowly and notice each foot lifting and landing.
Match your breathing to your pace without forcing it.
After a few steps, pause and notice how steady you feel.

title: Seated Stretch Reset
description: Gentle seated stretches to release tension.
steps: Sit upright and take one slow breath.
Lift your shoulders toward your ears and roll them back down.
Gently stretch your neck to each side without forcing it.
Reach your arms overhead, then lower them slowly and rest.

title: Mindful Listening
description: A practice that centers attention through sound.
steps: Sit quietly and close your eyes if that feels comfortable.
Notice the nearest sound you can hear.
Expand your attention to sounds farther away without judging them.
Return to one steady sound and follow it for one final breath.

title: Affirmation Breath
description: Pair a calming phrase with your breath.
steps: Choose a short phrase such as I am safe or I can slow down.
Breathe in gently and think the first half of the phrase.
Breathe out slowly and think the second half of the phrase.
Repeat for a few cycles and notice whether your body softens.

title: Stress Release Check-In
description: Notice, name, and soften what you are carrying.
steps: Pause and ask yourself where stress is showing up in your body.
Name the feeling in one word without judging it.
Take a slow exhale and imagine creating space around that feeling.
End by reminding yourself that this moment can pass.

title: Morning Intention
description: A simple intention-setting practice for the day.
steps: Take one full breath and notice how you are arriving in this moment.
Ask yourself how you want to feel today.
Choose one intention word such as steady, kind, or patient.
Repeat that word to yourself three times before continuing your day.

title: Sleep Wind Down
description: A quiet practice to prepare your body for rest.
steps: Dim your focus and settle into a comfortable resting position.
Take three slow breaths, each one a little longer on the exhale.
Let your jaw, shoulders, and hands soften.
Tell yourself: My body can rest now.
